data.body:
ROUND 7 — Joe's challenge batch: codebase verification + visual POC + web evidence.

RUN-STATE REUSE (Joe's "pattern matching vs reuse" challenge) — VERDICT: GENUINE REUSE, settled by code.
WORKFLOW_RUN/WORKFLOW_STEP are first-class event types (KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES _version.py:80-86; EVENT_TYPES
allow-list event_append.py). Emitted by TicketEventRecorder (executor.py:213-256) via _seam.append_event ->
event_append.write_and_push — the SAME locked write+push path as CREATE/EDIT/TRANSITION/COMMENT/TAG_DELTA.
Reduced by the SAME reducer (process_workflow_run/process_workflow_step, _processors.py:385-424, dispatched
:626-629 alongside all ticket events); compacted into SNAPSHOTs; per-key LWW + HLC-UUID order -> converges
across clones. Run-state is stored as events ON THE TARGET TICKET'S log; status/result READ via the same
reducer (show_ticket). So it is NOT a parallel store — the event-sourced SUBSTRATE (append/reduce/commit/
push/converge) is general-purpose; tickets are one schema on it, workflow run-state is another schema on the
SAME substrate, attached to the ticket the workflow operates on. Implications: a DURABLE run requires an
associated ticket (no ticket -> MemoryRecorder, ephemeral); run/step events kept OUT of hot paths via lazy
maps (setdefault), same discipline as session_log. Our new thin interpreter REUSES this recorder (extended
with iteration-keyed step markers per the engine POC) — extends existing reuse, not a new system.

DETERMINISTIC STEPS internal-only — CONFIRMED: STEP_REGISTRY (steps.py) is internal Python via @register_step;
NOT exported by the library, NOT in CLI, NOT an MCP tool. MCP exposes run_workflow (takes a YAML), never
individual steps. Matches Joe's requirement (deterministic logic internal, not client CLI/MCP surface).

LANGFUSE optional/client-light — CONFIRMED: in the [agents] extra (optional), lazy-imported inside _trace(),
no-op unless BOTH keys set (runner.py:527-550). Dev/observability only, not a client runtime dep. In the NEW
design it moves to a [tracing] OTel exporter (Langfuse = an OTLP endpoint) -> even lighter; clients who don't
set keys pay nothing.

OTel->LANGFUSE FIDELITY (Joe: find projects that confirmed it) — CONFIRMED SOLID: Langfuse has a dedicated
Pydantic AI integration guide + cookbook + OTLP endpoint (/api/public/otel); nested fidelity (agent run ->
LLM call -> tool span) confirmed by MAINTAINERS (pydantic-ai#3186 MCP-nesting fixed; #875 OTel-GenAI closed;
#3580 "everything working" vs Langfuse). Emitter is Logfire's OTel lib but Logfire SaaS is OPTIONAL (point
OTEL endpoint at Langfuse, send_to_logfire=false). CAVEAT (bounded): token/cost attribute mapping is the soft
spot (#2262/#4364 open) — affects cost analytics, NOT span structure. So dropping LangChain keeps Langfuse.

PYDANTIC AI MCP+FS+custom tools — CONFIRMED (docs): MCP via MCPServerStdio / MCPServerStreamableHTTP /
MCPServerSSE; custom tools via @agent.tool / @agent.tool_plain / tools=. FS = custom Python tools (reuse
rebar's fs_tools). Custom-tool use already validated LIVE on Anthropic in the runtime POC.

VISUAL POC (visual_bpmn_roundtrip_poc.mjs, bpmn-moddle ~15KB/3 deps/MIT — the HEADLESS layer, NOT the
browser editor): T1 PASS — with a registered `rebar` moddle descriptor, read/write/read preserves agent-step
metadata (prompt/provider/tools), all 11 stable ids, and multiInstance(fan-out). T2 (no descriptor) — unknown
extension is LAX-PRESERVED by bpmn-moddle (more robust than the forums implied); typed/guaranteed handling +
safety across bpmn-js editor save/copy cycles still wants the descriptor. T3 PASS — a simulated human edit
(rename + add a step) round-trips with the agent extension intact. => the IR<->BPMN serializer is FEASIBLE
and BOUNDED (~one moddle descriptor + round-trip fixtures).

DRAW.IO vs BPMN-JS (Joe's free-form concern) — VALIDATED + DECISIVE for bpmn-js. draw.io is free-form, no
domain metamodel, no validation ("you can't validate a drawing"; drawio->BPMN converters discard
non-conforming elements) -> arbitrary edits that don't map back = confusing/lossy UX (Joe's exact concern).
bpmn-js constrains editing to the BPMN metamodel (BpmnRules/canConnect; palette restricted; backed by
bpmn-moddle so it validates during modeling) -> edits can't drift outside the IR's vocabulary IF our IR maps
onto BPMN. => choose bpmn-js (constrained metamodel), NOT draw.io.

NOTE: bpmn-js (the EDITOR) is browser-bound (~50KB-hundreds-KB) + custom bpmn.io license (watermark); but the
EDITOR is an out-of-process edit-time tool, and the SERIALIZER only needs bpmn-moddle (MIT, light). DI/layout
must be generated (bpmn-auto-layout, all-or-nothing) — but since the visual format is EPHEMERAL we just
auto-layout on open; nothing persisted.

ALL 3 DE-RISK POCs now PASS: engine replay-correctness; runtime cross-provider+thinking-mitigation; visual
IR<->BPMN round-trip.
